Paper Title: How Good Are GPT Models at Machine Translation? A Comprehensive Evaluation
Authors: Amr Hendy, Mohamed Abdelrehim, Amr Sharaf, Vikas Raunak, Mohamed Gabr, Hitokazu Matsushita, Young Jin Kim, Mohamed Afify, Hany Hassan Awadalla
Summary: This paper evaluates the performance of GPT models in machine translation, involving random sampling during the human evaluation part to select 425 non-identical translation item pairs for each language pair.

The GPT-4 Technical Report discusses the random selection of three substrings of 50 characters for each evaluation example to match substrings between the evaluation and training data and identify potential contamination.
Reason for Selection: Random selection for evaluation example contamination check

Paper Title: Can GPT Improve the State of Prior Authorization via Guideline Based Automated Question Answering?
Summary: This study explores the use of GPT to answer questions about patient data from electronic health records, conducting a qualitative analysis on 20 randomly picked patient health records.
Reason for Selection: Qualitative analysis on randomly picked patient health records
